data.title: Cooper Companies cut to Equal Weight at Wells Fargo on segment challenges
data.description:
The Cooper Companies (COO) was downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight at Wells Fargo, amid mounting signs of a slowdown in the company’s core markets.

The decision reflects a belief that the contact lens market is losing momentum and that Cooper is less likely to outperform peers or the broader market in the near term.

Wells Fargo anticipates that Cooper’s CooperVision segment will deliver growth roughly in line with the market at about 3% in the fiscal fourth quarter, rather than materially outperforming.

Wells Fargo analysts also point to continued weakness in CooperSurgical’s in vitro fertilization business, which accounts for roughly 40% of the segment’s revenue and about 13% of total company sales.

Wells Fargo has a price target of $72.49 for the company.

data.title: FlyExclusive outlines sustained profitability path as fleet modernization slashes nonperforming aircraft losses by 85%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: flyExclusive (FLYX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Jim Segrave, Founder, CEO & Chairman of the Board, stated that "the third quarter marked another very strong step forward for flyExclusive. Our transformation is clearly working. Our strategy is delivering results and the positive impact is accelerating across the business." He highlighted key achievements such as cost reduction, sales growth, increased membership, higher utilization, and improved financial performance.

  • Segrave detailed progress in fleet modernization, reporting the elimination of 26 nonperforming aircraft over the last 12 months, including 2 more in Q3 and 2 in Q4 so far. The operational drag from these aircraft was reduced by roughly 85%, lowering monthly losses from over $3 million per month in 2024 to just under $0.5 million per month. He emphasized, "At its peak, our nonperforming fleet represented an annualized EBITDA drag of roughly $36 million. That drag is nearly gone, and our financial results showed just how dramatic this transformation has improved our performance."

  • Segrave noted that fleet utilization approached 7,000 hours in October, the largest month in company history, and stated, "We now have 7 challengers in operation and 2 more in the immediate pipeline."

  • The company reported a 20% year-over-year revenue increase to $92 million for the quarter, with roughly half now contracted through partner, fractional, and Jet Club programs. Contractually committed hours grew 30% compared to Q3 '24. Segrave also said, "Retail membership grew 51% year-over-year, a testament to our brand momentum and service reliability."

  • The Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul (MRO) business saw 103% year-over-year revenue growth in Q3, and Segrave pointed to external demand and expanded internal throughput as drivers. He added, "We have added 6 additional mobile service units in October, bringing the total to 12."

  • Segrave stated, "Year-to-date, gross profit increased 82% year-over-year and gross margin expanded by roughly 500 basis points. Adjusted EBITDA improved 72% and adjusted EBITDAR increased 104% year-over-year."

  • Bradley Garner, Chief Financial Officer, commented, "From top line growth, operational discipline and marked bottom line improvement, third quarter illustrated what we believe to be a sustainable and accelerating path towards profitability and scale."

  • Garner reported, "Revenue for the third quarter totaled $92.1 million, which is a 20% increase over Q3 of 2024. Year-to-date revenue expanded 15% to $272 million compared to the same period last year. Impressively, and largely as a result of our fleet modernization initiative, we accomplished this growth with a fleet that is 20% smaller than it was a year ago."

  • Garner added that the company ended Q3 with more than 1,160 retail members, and Jet Club program retail sales exceeded $31 million during the quarter, up roughly 4% year-over-year. "Fractional sales were up 68% year-to-date compared to last year, fueled by growing demand for the Challenger platform and reinforced by confirmation of 100% bonus depreciation in the latest tax legislation."

  • The CFO also highlighted the acquisition of Volato's aircraft sales division for $2.1 million in stock, expected to generate $6 million to $8 million in profit in Q4 2025, and noted the right to acquire additional technology platforms for $2 million in stock.

Outlook

  • Segrave expressed confidence in delivering the best performance yet to close out 2025, stating, "Given the efficiency gains achieved so far and the strength of our core programs, we expect our fourth quarter performance to continue to reflect the positive trajectory we've demonstrated all year, both operationally and financially."

  • He added, "We expect to reduce the number of nonperforming aircraft to mid-single digits by the end of 2025 and to fully eliminate it in 2026."

  • Garner said, "We expect that mix [contractually committed revenue] to continue to trend higher as these programs scale."

  • Both executives communicated sustained positive adjusted EBITDA going into 2026 and beyond.

Financial Results

  • The company reported third quarter revenue of $92.1 million, a 20% increase year-over-year. Year-to-date revenue reached $272 million, up 15% compared to the same period last year.

  • Gross margin increased 46% compared to Q3 2024, ending the third quarter at 14%. Year-to-date, gross profit increased 82%.

  • The adjusted EBITDA loss for Q3 2025 was $1.9 million, compared to a $13 million loss in Q3 of last year.

  • Wholesale flight revenue totaled $47.5 million in Q3 2025, a 15% increase compared to Q3 last year. Year-to-date wholesale revenue grew 4% to over $134 million.

  • The MRO business generated external revenue of $3.1 million in Q3 2025, and $7.7 million year-to-date, surpassing 2024's full year revenue.

  • SG&A as a percentage of revenue declined nearly 500 basis points year-over-year to $19.5 million.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The transcript does not include a Q&A session or analyst questions, so no exchanges between analysts and management are available for review.

Sentiment Analysis

  • The tone of analyst sentiment cannot be assessed due to the absence of a Q&A session in the transcript.

  • Management's tone throughout the prepared remarks was confident and optimistic, emphasizing phrases like "we are now operating from a position of sustained strength" and "the heavy lifting of our transformation is behind us."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone maintained its positive momentum, with increased emphasis on the sustainability and durability of recent improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize sustained positive adjusted EBITDA into 2026, compared to prior focus on approaching breakeven.

  • Management highlighted a further reduction in nonperforming aircraft and a continued increase in contractually committed revenue, whereas last quarter focused more on the initial impact of Challenger fleet additions.

  • The expansion of the MRO business and acquisition of Volato's aircraft sales division mark new strategic advancements not previously emphasized.

  • SG&A improvements and gross margin expansion continued, but this quarter saw a stronger narrative around scalability and operational leverage.

  • Analyst sentiment cannot be compared due to the absence of a Q&A in both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged delays in the Jet.AI merger and ATM sales facility finalization due to the Federal government shutdown.

  • Garner described the Jet.AI transaction as "not only provide operational synergies with the acquisition of their aviation operations, but will provide capital for growth and delevering of our balance sheet."

  • The company highlighted ongoing efforts to improve liquidity and balance sheet flexibility.

Final Takeaway

flyExclusive management emphasized that the company has achieved significant operational and financial improvements through its fleet modernization and disciplined cost structure. The near elimination of nonperforming aircraft and the expansion of high-performing jets have driven revenue and margin growth, while MRO operations and membership programs continue to expand. Management expressed confidence in delivering record results to close out 2025 and sustaining positive adjusted EBITDA into 2026, signaling a new phase of growth and profitability for the company.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Siemens slides on earnings concerns; sees currency headwinds hurting results
data.description:
Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) -6.7% in Frankfurt trading on Thursday, dropping from a record closing high in the previous session, after reporting mixed FQ4 results that included higher revenues but lower net profit that missed consensus.

Q4 revenues rose 6% Y/Y on a comparable basis to €21.43 billion, in line with analyst estimates provided by the company, as comparable orders rose in all industrial businesses except Mobility, which had recorded a sharply higher volume from large orders in Q4 2024; as a result, orders for Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) overall came in 1% lower Y/Y to €21.94 billion but narrowly beat analyst consensus.

Q4 net profit fell 13% Y/Y to €1.84 billion, below the €2.14 billion consensus; industrial profit rose slightly to €3.19 billion, short of the €3.32 billion consensus, with a narrowed margin of 15.3% from 15.5%; free cash flow from continuing and discontinued operations of €5.3 billion reached the company's highest level ever achieved in a quarter.

Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) guided for FY 2026 earnings of €10.40-€11.00/share, missing estimates of €11.54/share, according to a company compiled consensus, warning that currency effects will hurt its volume growth rates, as well as profit from industrial businesses; comparable revenue growth is forecast at 6%-8% for FY 2026 and 6%-9% in the medium term.

The company proposed a dividend of €5.35/share, up from €5.20/share.

Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) CEO Roland Busch rejected analyst concerns that the mid-term 6%-9% revenue growth target, which excludes the impact of takeovers and Healthineers, was too conservative.

"We are in a good place because we are offering what the world needs more and more of," the CEO said, according to Reuters, pointing to growth driven by strong demand in rail, aerospace and defense, and data centers and artificial intelligence.

Busch said the reduction of Siemens' (OTCPK:SIEGY) holding in Healthineers allows the company to focus on its core areas of factory and building automation as well as its train manufacturing.

data.title: Bitfarms winds down bitcoin mining, looking to more profitable HPC/AI
data.description:
Bitfarms (BITF) is winding down its bitcoin mining operations, in favor of focusing on high-performance compute/artificial intelligence infrastructure, it said on Thursday. The announcement when it said it's converting its Washington site to support HPC/AI workloads.

"We believe that the conversion of just our Washington site to GPU-as-a-Service could potentially  produce more net operating income than we have ever generated with bitcoin mining, providing the company with a strong cashflow foundation that could fund opex, G&A, and debt service and contribute to capex as we wind down our bitcoin mining business in 2026 and 2027," CEO Ben Gagnon said in a statement.

Bitfarms (BITF) stock dropped 11% in Thursday midmorning trading. The company also posted a bigger-than-expected loss in Q3 2025 amid the strategy shift.

data.title: xAI raises $15B in latest funding round to support GPU purchases: report
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk's artificial intelligence company, xAI (X.AI), has raised $15B in its latest funding round, according to a report by CNBC.

The funding round had garnered $10B by September, which propelled xAI to a valuation of $200B. It has since raised another $5B, elevating the current round to $15B. The report said much of the funds raised will be directed for the purchase of graphics processing units, or GPUs.    

xAI uses GPUs from both Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD). xAI's Colossus supercomputer in Memphis, Tenn., contains 200,000 GPUs, but xAI has said it has a roadmap for 1M GPUs.

xAI is the creator of the Grok large language model.

data.title: Kura Oncology wins FDA approval for leukemia drug
data.description:
• The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Thursday approved ziftomenib (Komzifti), a leukemia drug developed by Kura Oncology (KURA) with Japanese drugmaker Kyowa Kirin (OTCPK:KYKOF) (OTCPK:KYKOY), triggering a trading halt in the California-based biotech.
  • In a press release, the FDA said it has approved the menin inhibitor for adults with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia ((AML)) with a nucleophosmin 1 (NPM1) genetic mutation for whom satisfactory alternative treatments are not available.
  • Previously, Kura’s (KURA) new drug application for ziftomenib was under the FDA’s priority review with a target action date of November 30.
  • “Bolstered by a strong balance sheet and our productive partnership with Kyowa Kirin, we are well-positioned to advance ziftomenib toward commercialization,” CEO Troy Wilson said with the company’s Q3 results last week.
